mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 640i engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 640i engine costs £4,500–£8,000 supply only depending on generation. F06/F12/F13 N55 engines start around £4,500; G32 B58 units start around £4,800. Fitting adds £1,200–£1,800. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

It depends on which generation you have. The N55 (F06/F12/F13) is a proven engine — reliable but with known weak points (HPFP, chargepipe, oil filter housing). The B58 (G32) is more modern and generally more robust, with improved cooling and forged internals, though PCV valve issues and oil filter housing leaks are noted. Neither is a disaster; both demand good maintenance.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 640i?

acceptedAnswer.text: F06/F12/F13 N55: HPFP failure, chargepipe failure (plastic cracks), oil filter housing leaks, water pump failure. G32 B58: PCV valve diaphragm failure, oil filter housing leaks, turbocharger wastegate rattle, coolant loss. The specific issues depend entirely on which generation you have.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 640i engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and which generation. Against a well-kept £18,000 640i, a £6,000 recon is 33% — worth it. Against a tired £8,000 example, the same recon approaches our 70% BMW threshold — get a scrap valuation first.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 640i engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. F06/F12/F13 (2011–2018): N55B30A. G32 (2017–2019): B58B30. The chassis code is the easiest tell — F06 is the Gran Coupe, F12 is the Convertible, F13 is the Coupe; G32 is the Gran Turismo.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the HPFP on the N55, or does it need full engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — HPFP replacement is a well-understood repair costing £600–£1,200. It's the N55's most common weak point. If you experience long cranking or hesitation, don't ignore it — but it's a straightforward fix, not an engine killer. For the B58, similar principles apply — PCV repair is affordable and well-documented.

eraMap.title: The 640i THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: The original 640i — a proven N55. HPFP, chargepipe, and oil filter housing are the known weak points.
The later B58 — more robust, with improved cooling and forged internals. PCV and oil filter housing are the main concerns.

eraMap.sourceNote: This table is the single source every other section on this page matches — Sections 2, 4 and 6 reference it rather than restating conflicting figures.

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the B58 and N55 3.0-litre turbo petrol engines. Used engines are available from approximately £3,500–£5,500, with reconditioned units ranging from £4,500–£8,000. N55 engines (F06/F12/F13) typically sit in the lower end of the range; B58 engines (G32) are generally more expensive.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a 640i engine typically takes 12–16 hours at £70–£100/hr (independent) or £120–£180/hr (London/SE dealer).

problems.title: High-Pressure Fuel Pump (HPFP) Failure — N55 (F06/F12/F13)
problems.symptoms: Long cranking on startup, hesitation under acceleration, engine stalling, engine warning light, reduced power.
problems.recommendation: The N55's HPFP is its most notorious weak point. If you experience long cranking or hesitation, don't ignore it — a failing HPFP can lead to lean running and catalytic converter damage. The replacement is well-understood and well-documented. This applies exclusively to the F06/F12/F13 640i with the N55 engine.

failureLink.label: Read the full HPFP failure guide

problems.title: Chargepipe Failure — N55 (F06/F12/F13)
problems.symptoms: Loss of boost, whistling noise from the engine bay, engine warning light, reduced power.
problems.recommendation: The N55's plastic chargepipe is prone to cracking, especially with age and heat cycles. The factory plastic pipes are a common failure point. The solution is an aftermarket metal chargepipe — fit once and forget. This is exclusively an F06/F12/F13 issue; the G32 B58 uses a revised design.

failureLink.label: Read the full chargepipe failure guide

problems.title: PCV Valve Failure — B58 (G32)
problems.symptoms: Rough idle, whistling noise from the engine, white or blue smoke from exhaust on startup, oil in the intake/chargepipe, misfires.
problems.repairCost: £200–£500 (PCV diaphragm repair kit)【PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet】
problems.recommendation: The PCV diaphragm on first-generation B58 engines can tear, causing high crankcase vacuum and smoking. Signs include misfires, a rough idle, and oil in the intake/chargepipe. The good news is this is easily fixed with an affordable repair kit. If you notice rough idle, whistling, or smoke on startup, have the PCV system inspected. This applies to the G32 640i with the B58 engine.

failureLink.label: Read the full PCV failure guide

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 640i? Check These First
buyIf: * F06/F12/F13: Documented HPFP and chargepipe history (aftermarket metal chargepipe preferred)
* F06/F12/F13: Full service history with BMW LL-01 approved oil
* G32: Full service history and no evidence of PCV or cooling system issues
* Any generation: No cold-start rattle, misfires, or hesitation
avoidIf: * F06/F12/F13: No proof of HPFP or chargepipe maintenance — these are whens, not ifs
* F06/F12/F13: Poor service history — the N55 is unforgiving of neglect
* G32: Rough idle, whistling, or smoke (PCV diaphragm warning)
* Any generation: Engine warning light with no diagnosis

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 640i badge spans two of BMW's finest modern straight-sixes — the proven N55 and the improved B58. The N55 is a legend with known weak points; the B58 is more robust but still has its own patterns. Both are rewarding engines when properly maintained. Buy the generation that matches your priorities — but buy on documented history either way. A well-maintained 640i is a £15,000–£30,000 grand tourer; a neglected one is a £7,000 engine replacement waiting to happen.

cta.label: → Compare 640i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 640i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling or hesitation (N55: HPFP warning; B58: PCV warning)
* Service History — including which engine era (N55 for F06/F12/F13, B58 for G32) and confirmed oil-interval discipline
* Chargepipe Check (N55) — Has the plastic chargepipe been replaced with an aftermarket metal unit?
* HPFP Check (N55) — Any history of long cranking, hesitation, or HPFP replacement?
* Smoke Check — White or blue smoke on startup can indicate PCV or turbo issues
* Oil Leak Check — Any history of oil leaks? (oil filter housing is a known weak point on both)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved HPFP, chargepipe, PCV, or cooling system issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
